physicalDescription.extent: lxx preliminary pages and 150 numbered pages

entities.label: Hermann Varnhagen, The Harrowing of Hell (1898)

physicalDescription.extent: 1 leaf + 34 pages + 1 leaf, within a university program
notes: Printed pp. 6–21 place diplomatic transcriptions of the three Harrowing witnesses in parallel; pp. 22–34 discuss their relationship. This is not a photographic manuscript facsimile.

next_steps: Collate Hulme's printed line numbering, the XML L elements, Varnhagen's diplomatic edition, and current manuscript-project counts.
Record any normalization only as an explicit editorial mapping, not as a replacement for source-specific counts.
notes: The XML has O 256, L 248, and E 200 line elements; historical prose summaries and current project records do not all use the same count.
openQuestions.question: How should differing modern line counts and lineation systems for the three Harrowing witnesses be reconciled without erasing editorial differences?

next_steps: Consult codicological studies and NLS conservation documentation for the f. 35/37 stub sequence.
Retain the current project's uncertainty as ff. ?35rb–?37rb or 37va stub until stronger evidence resolves it.
openQuestions.question: What exact textual boundary should be assigned to the incomplete Auchinleck Harrowing around the damaged stubs and lost leaves?

next_steps: Monitor the exact Archives and Manuscripts records and British Library digital-service recovery notices.
Ask the British Library about mediated full-manuscript files or whole-object reproduction orders for all four witnesses.
If only selected images are supplied, mark them partial and do not substitute them for a complete physical-object sequence.
openQuestions.question: When will Harley MS 2253's full digital coverage return, and can the British Library provide complete digital objects for Cotton MS Galba E IX, Harley MS 4196, and Add MS 32578?

next_steps: Ask the University of Michigan Digital Content and Collections unit for production notes or source-package documentation for CME00010.
Preserve the observable source comparison separately from any future explanation of why the selection was made.
openQuestions.question: What project documentation records the exact local decisions that selected title matter and printed pp. 2–136 while omitting the 1907 introduction, glossary, and index?

next_steps: Ask Lambeth Palace Library to expand the LUNA rights value 'LPL' for this object and distinguish copyright from contractual reproduction permission.
Continue to treat publication reuse as permission-required until item-level terms are supplied.
openQuestions.question: What item-level copyright and reuse terms govern the publicly delivered Sion L40.2/E25 compound-book images?

reviewNotes: The seven DIV2 units are witness texts in a parallel-witness scholarly edition, not seven intrinsic chapters or seven independent abstract works.
Printed pages 22–23 are shared physical surfaces: the final Harrowing columns overlap the first Gospel columns, so the work boundary cannot be represented as a simple page break.
Complete facsimile coverage means the provider's complete publicly exposed physical-object sequence. Work-only leaves are recorded separately in local_copies[].work_portion.
The Auchinleck bundle is complete for the project's 701 public codex and detached-fragment surfaces; it does not assert that covers, spine, edges, or preservation masters are publicly exposed.

lineage.summary: CME00010 is a manually keyed University of Michigan encoding of selected matter from William Henry Hulme's 1907 EETS edition: three separately lineated manuscript versions of The Harrowing of Hell and four separately lineated manuscript versions of the strophic Gospel of Nicodemus, with interleaved editorial notes. The XML does not encode the edition's preface, introduction, glossary, index, or outer series matter. The source edition and complete publicly obtainable digital objects for Bodleian MS Digby 86, the Auchinleck manuscript's exposed 701-image project inventory, and Lambeth Sion L40.2/E25 are cached locally; current British Library records do not provide obtainable complete image objects for the remaining four BL witnesses, and Harley MS 2253's former full digital coverage is currently unavailable.

catalogSummary.author: Anonymous Middle English poets and translator-adapters
catalogSummary.date: Harrowing composed c. 1200–1250; Gospel composed c. 1300–1350; edition first published 1907
catalogSummary.region: England; the Gospel's authorial dialect is Northern English
catalogSummary.title: The Middle-English Harrowing of Hell and Gospel of Nicodemus

catalogingSubject.label: The Middle-English Harrowing of Hell and Gospel of Nicodemus
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The edition-level compilation represented by University of Michigan CME item CME00010: William Henry Hulme's seven separately presented manuscript versions of two anonymous Middle English verse works, together with encoded title matter and interleaved editorial apparatus from the 1907 EETS edition. This record does not collapse the two medieval works, their seven witness versions, the physical manuscripts, the complete 1907 volume, the U-M encoding, or the repository XML into one abstract medieval work.

contentParts.label: Encoded title matter and editorial apparatus

locators.value: main title; part title; local apparatus on pp. 2–136
front matter, DIV1/DIV2 headings, 431 NOTE elements

contentParts.summary: Main title page, Harrowing part title, witness labels, page summaries, line/stanza numbering, and interleaved textual notes retained from Hulme's edition; the long introduction and back matter are omitted.

contentParts.label: The Harrowing of Hell

locators.value: pp. 2–23, parallel columns; p. 23 shared with Gospel opening
DIV1 The Harrowing of Hell; DIV2 1–3

contentParts.summary: Three separately lineated short-couplet manuscript versions, conventionally identified as O, L, and E.

contentParts.label: Digby MS, text O

dateStatements.display: Hulme's edition-level compilation was first published in 1907
notes: This is the publication date of the cataloging subject, not a medieval composition date.

dateStatements.display: The Harrowing of Hell was probably composed c. 1200–1250; PCMEP suggests c. 1240
The earliest surviving Harrowing witness represented here is MS Digby 86, dated 1272–1282
The Strophic Gospel of Nicodemus was probably composed c. 1300–1350; PCMEP suggests c. 1340
The earliest surviving Gospel witnesses represented here are dated to the early 15th century, with Cotton MS Galba E IX cataloged to the first quarter of the century

extent.description: Seven XML DIV2 witness texts grouped under two medieval works: three Harrowing versions (704 L elements) and four Gospel versions (7,024 L elements in 586 LG stanza groups), plus encoded title matter and 431 NOTE elements. These are representation counts, not normalized canonical line totals.

formStatements.description: Verse witness texts with prose title matter, notes, headings, and editorial apparatus

scope.description: Rubrics, speech labels, explicit, and short phrases embedded in the Middle English witness texts

notes: This record describes an edition-level compilation and gives the two medieval works and seven witness versions explicit part boundaries.
The XML's 7,728 L elements and 586 LG elements are representation metrics. They must not be presented as normalized critical counts without reconciling source-specific lineation.
Printed pp. 22–23 are shared by columns belonging to the ending Harrowing versions and opening Gospel versions; the physical edition boundary is overlapping, not a clean page break.
Hulme's preface says the volume edits the poetical Gospel versions; prose Gospel witnesses are bibliographic context for a deferred volume and are outside CME00010's content boundary.
Manuscript production dates, origins, and image rights remain in the linked lineage manifest rather than being transferred wholesale into abstract work metadata.

next_steps: Collate source-specific numbering without replacing any source's own lineation with an invented canonical total.
openQuestions.question: How should the differing XML, Hulme, Varnhagen, and current project line counts for the Harrowing witnesses be crosswalked?

next_steps: Consult codicological and conservation studies; retain ?35rb–?37rb or 37va stub until stronger evidence resolves it.
openQuestions.question: Can the incomplete Auchinleck Harrowing's exact boundary around ff. 35–37 and the damaged stubs be resolved?

next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for item-level production notes while preserving the directly observed source comparison.
openQuestions.question: What U-M production record explains the exact selection of title matter and pp. 2–136 and omission of the introduction, glossary, and index?

next_steps: Monitor exact BL records and ask about mediated whole-manuscript copies or reproduction orders; keep any selected images explicitly partial.
openQuestions.question: When will Harley MS 2253's complete images return, and can complete digital objects be obtained for Cotton Galba E IX, Harley 4196, and Add 32578?

notes: This broad origin does not transfer any witness's copying place to the lost authorial text.
London is not asserted as the composition place of either medieval work.

responsibilities.agentName: Anonymous author of The Harrowing of Hell
Anonymous Middle English translator-adapter of the Strophic Gospel of Nicodemus
notes: The role describes the anonymous Middle English rhymed rendition of the apocryphal Latin tradition, not a claim that Nicodemus personally composed the English poem.

scope.description: All seven separately printed witness texts and their apparatus

summaries.value: A seven-witness scholarly compilation of two anonymous Middle English verse works. Three short-couplet Harrowing versions dramatize Christ's descent to hell and liberation of the righteous. Four strophic Gospel versions narrate the trial, Passion, Resurrection, and descent tradition associated with the apocryphal Gospel of Nicodemus. Hulme prints the witnesses separately, largely follows manuscript readings, and supplies interleaved textual notes.

CME00010 contains the main title, Harrowing part title, seven witness-text divisions, and local apparatus corresponding to printed pp. 2–136. It omits the 1907 preface and introduction (pp. i–lxx), glossary (pp. 137–148), index (pp. 149–150), and outer series matter. The prose Gospel versions listed in Hulme's introduction were deferred and are not part of the edited text or this encoding.

titles.value: The Middle-English Harrowing of Hell and Gospel of Nicodemus

notes: The title's claim to include an introduction and glossary describes the complete 1907 volume; those sections are not encoded in CME00010.
titles.value: The Middle-English Harrowing of Hell and Gospel of Nicodemus. Now First Edited from All the Known Manuscripts, with Introduction and Glossary

notes: The supplied qualifier 'Strophic' distinguishes this verse rendition from the prose Gospel witnesses discussed but not edited by Hulme.

work.author: Anonymous Middle English poets and translator-adapters

Three short-couplet Harrowing versions dramatize Christ's descent to hell and liberation of the righteous. Four strophic Gospel versions narrate the trial, Passion, Resurrection, and descent tradition associated with the apocryphal Gospel of Nicodemus. Hulme prints the witnesses separately, largely follows manuscript readings, and supplies interleaved textual notes.
